checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?

evidence.quote: AccountsReceivableNet=2547679092.8 (2010-12-31) -> AccountsReceivableNet=3035651852.44 (2011-12-31), a +19.2% increase, while Revenues=14202843354.0 (2010) -> Revenues=15586529309.87 (2011), a +9.7% increase

AccountsReceivableNet=3035651852.44 (2011-12-31) -> AccountsReceivableNet=3530177140.69 (2012-12-31), a +16.3% increase, while Revenues=15586529309.87 (2011) -> Revenues=16949244920.49 (2012), a +8.7% increase
Note: this reverses sharply in FY2013 - AccountsReceivableNet=2840743705.39 (2013-12-31) declines -19.5% while Revenues=20230204363.38 (2013) grows +19.4%, tempering the concern

checklist.question: Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.quote: Goodwill=853881432.85 (2012-09-30) jumps to Goodwill=2496496501.1 (2012-12-31), roughly a 192% quarter-over-quarter increase
By contrast, quarterly Revenues actually declined slightly: Revenues=4727436239.06 (Q3 2012) vs Revenues=4356520675.25 (Q4 2012)

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss=-236676056.62 (2009) vs NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities=3180134263.35 (2009), a large positive divergence likely driven by heavy D&A typical of capital-intensive operations

checklist.question: Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: InventoryNet=175438366.55 (2009-12-31) to InventoryNet=173344336.91 (2013-12-31), essentially flat, while Revenues grew from 13337009988.76 (2009) to 20230204363.38 (2013), a ~52% increase
No CostOfGoodsSold or gross margin concept was provided in the dataset, limiting direct margin-trend assessment
evidence.location: absence of COGS/gross margin series in financial_series_point_in_time

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: Quarterly NetIncomeLoss/ProfitLoss shows material swings including losses: ProfitLoss=-242159972.8 (Q1 2011), ProfitLoss=-105796387.12 (Q1 2012), ProfitLoss=-69112368.34 implied via NetIncomeLossAvailableToCommonStockholdersBasic (Q4 2012)

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: Liabilities=26114292428.29 (2009-12-31) grew to Liabilities=40973868550.27 (2013-12-31), roughly tracking or exceeding Revenue growth from 13337009988.76 (2009) to 20230204363.38 (2013)
No AllowanceForDoubtfulAccounts, bad debt reserve, or specific warranty/litigation reserve concept was provided in the dataset, preventing a granular reserve-adequacy assessment
evidence.location: absence of allowance/reserve series in financial_series_point_in_time

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: NT 10-K filed 2009-03-03, NT 10-K filed 2013-03-04, and NT 10-K filed 2014-03-03 - three late-filing notifications within the observed window

10-K filed 2014-03-19 followed immediately by 10-K/A filed 2014-03-20 (accession 0001364479-14-000008), a one-day turnaround amendment carrying materially different Goodwill, Intangibles, Assets, Liabilities and Debt balances for FY2012 and FY2013
10-K filed 2010-02-26 was followed by a 10-K/A filed just three days later on 2010-03-01

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Core series (Revenues, NetIncomeLoss/ProfitLoss, AccountsReceivableNet, Assets, Liabilities, Goodwill, IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill, InventoryNet, PropertyPlantAndEquipment, StockholdersEquity) span 2007-2013 with quarterly and annual granularity, providing a reasonably long baseline

However, NetCashProvidedByUsedInFinancingActivities and NetCashProvidedByUsedInInvestingActivities data end at 2011-03-31, and no AllowanceForDoubtfulAccounts or CostOfGoodsSold concepts are provided, limiting full assessment of CL3, CL4, and CL6 in later periods

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Possible purchase price allocation issue, business combination accounting error, or restated acquisition accounting recognized between the original 10-K (filed 2014-03-19) and the 10-K/A (filed one day later on 2014-03-20), producing an abrupt, disproportionate jump in Goodwill and Intangibles relative to underlying revenue in Q4 2012, which was then carried through the FY2013 comparative balances.

rationale_evidence.quote: 10-K filed 2014-03-19 immediately amended by 10-K/A filed 2014-03-20 carrying these same restated FY2012/FY2013 balances

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Potential premature or aggressive revenue recognition (or delayed write-off/reserving) in FY2011-FY2012, causing receivables to build up faster than sales growth, followed by a sharp catch-up decline in FY2013 potentially reflecting write-offs, reserve adjustments, or improved collections tied to the restatement.

rationale_evidence.quote: AccountsReceivableNet=3035651852.44 (2011-12-31) growing +19.2% year-over-year vs Revenues growth of +9.7% (Revenues=14202843354.0 in 2010 to Revenues=15586529309.87 in 2011)
AccountsReceivableNet=3530177140.69 (2012-12-31) growing +16.3% vs Revenues growth of +8.7%

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Chronic delays in financial statement completion (evidenced by repeated NT 10-K notices) combined with rapid post-filing amendments suggest weak internal controls over financial close, raising the risk that balance sheet and income statement items across multiple periods were subject to late correction/reclassification rather than being accurately reported the first time.

rationale_evidence.quote: 10-K/A filed 2010-03-01, only three days after the original 10-K filed 2010-02-26
10-K/A filed 2014-03-20, one day after the original 10-K filed 2014-03-19, carrying materially revised balances

top_signals: Repeated NT 10-K late-filing notices in 2009, 2013, and 2014 combined with a 10-K/A filed just one day after the original 10-K on 2014-03-20, indicating recurring reporting/control weaknesses

Disproportionate one-time jump in Goodwill (from 853881432.85 to 2496496501.1) and Intangibles (from 4754333721.11 to 7568931165.84) in Q4 2012 embedded in the restated 10-K/A, far outpacing revenue growth
Accounts receivable growing materially faster than revenue in FY2011 and FY2012 (consecutive years), followed by an abrupt reversal in FY2013
Persistent large multiple (5x-11x) between reported net income/profit and operating cash flow across all observed years, though partly explainable by capital intensity
